summary: Large cross-directory decomp matching cleanup PR spanning Dolphin THP, SysDolphin baselib, camera, fighters, game modes, stages, items, menus, libraries, map/platform code, trophies, and VI. The PR reported +0.31% matched code (+11944 bytes), +0.04% matched data (+448 bytes), 26 new matches, 79 unmatched-item improvements, and no broken/fuzzy/metric regressions after rebasing to origin/master 07bb71f. It was closed unmerged because the work was superseded by smaller directory-specific draft PRs #2619-#2630 and #2632.
what_changed: Improved source shape across 67 files with 941 additions and 757 deletions, yielding 26 newly fully matched items and 79 improvements in previously unmatched items according to the PR report.

Reworked THP decompression source shape in extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c, including using a local THPFileInfo* info consistently and replacing raw offset-derived mcuBuffer access with THPFileInfoMCUBufferView casts.
Adjusted Camera_80029CF8 and Camera_80030CFC source shape in src/melee/cm/camera.c using temporary variables, explicit __assert calls instead of HSD_ASSERTMSG macros, and stack padding changes.
Added static HSD_TevDesc and HSD_TECnst data definitions in src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c, completing that file's .data match per the report.

Improved fighter files through variable ordering, helper reuse, explicit temporaries, stack padding, type refinement, and replacing M2C_FIELD-style access with typed struct field access where evidence appears in the diff.
Improved game-mode logic in files such as gm_1601.c by inlining return expressions and replacing a manual unlock-character search with fn_801605EC.

Touched many stage, item, menu, library, trophy, and SysDolphin units to improve small residual code/data mismatches, with gricemt, particle, mncount, mnitemsw, gm_18A5, gm_16F1, grmutecity, and itseakchain among the largest file deltas.
The PR itself was not merged; comments state it was superseded by directory split drafts for Dolphin support, CM, FT, GM, GR, IF, IT, LB, MN, MP, PL, TY, and VI.
smart_moves: Kept the regression story explicit: git diff --check, fresh baseline via ninja baseline, clean temp-worktree regression gate via ninja changes_all, and reported zero broken matches, fuzzy regressions, and metric regressions.
Split a very broad matching sweep into smaller directory-specific draft PRs after the initial aggregate PR, making review and integration more tractable.
Used typed overlay/view structs for awkward binary layout access in THPDec instead of preserving raw pointer arithmetic where the source shape could still match.
Recovered data matches by adding concrete static data objects, as shown by ftmaterial .data moving to 100%.
Preferred existing helpers and typed fields where they produced better matching/readability, for example ftCo_800A1AB4 for distance logic and Fighter_804D64FC->x20 instead of M2C_FIELD.
Used explicit __assert calls with known string/data symbols in camera code where macro expansion shape apparently did not match.
decomp_lessons: For broad matching work, produce a regression report with both new full matches and partial unmatched-item improvements; small source-shape changes across many files can add up to meaningful matched-code gains.
When a PR spans many subsystems, split by directory or subsystem before review. This PR was closed specifically because it was superseded by smaller split draft PRs.
Typed view structs can replace raw offset pointer casts while preserving layout-sensitive access patterns, as in THPFileInfoMCUBufferView for mcuBuffer.
Local variable declaration order, explicit temporaries, and PAD_STACK placement remain important matching levers in Metrowerks-style decompilation.
Macro convenience may hurt matching: replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with explicit conditionals and __assert calls can recover exact codegen when string/data symbol placement matters.
Data-section progress may require reconstructing static descriptors/constants, not only function-body edits.
Replacing manual repeated code with an already-matching inline/helper can improve both readability and codegen, but should be backed by regression checks.
naming_conventions: Subsystem prefixes follow existing project style: Camera_, ftCo_, ftMaterial_, gm_, grIceMt_, grBigBlue_, it_, mnItemSw_, lb_, HSD_, and THP-related __THP names.
Unidentified or local functions retain address-style names such as fn_80197AF0, fn_800D8EC8, and hsd_80395D88 when stronger names are not evidenced.
Static data symbols use file/subsystem plus address-style names, e.g. ftMaterial_803C69D0 and ftMaterial_803C6A44.
Fighter typed aliases and structs are preferred when available, e.g. ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs, ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t, Fighter_x1A88_t.
Avoid raw M2C_FIELD expressions when a validated typed field exists, as shown by Fighter_804D64FC->x20.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use local pointer aliases for globals where it improves register allocation/source shape, e.g. THPFileInfo* info = __THPInfo and then info->xPixelSize/components/quantTabs.
Introduce or remove temporaries to control expression evaluation order and generated instructions, e.g. camera pitch_angle computation using temp_f31.
Adjust declaration ordering and delayed initialization to match stack/register allocation, as seen in ftCo_Attack100 and camera changes.
Use PAD_STACK and explicit dummy padding variables to tune stack frame size and local layout.
Replace macro assertions with explicit if-not condition and __assert(file, line, message) when matching requires direct calls and specific string symbols.
Use typed overlay casts for known packed/layout-sensitive fields instead of ad hoc byte offsets, as with THPFileInfoMCUBufferView and THPRestartFields.
Substitute literal zero float with an extern sdata symbol when needed for data/reference matching, as shown by ftCo_804D9018 in ftCo_800D730C.
Inline or de-inline helper sequences selectively, such as replacing a sequence of ftCo_800B46B8 calls with ftCo_800AD54C_inline0.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.

Author comments state the PR was superseded by directory split drafts: #2632 for Dolphin support; #2619 CM; #2620 FT; #2621 GM; #2622 GR; #2623 IF; #2624 IT; #2625 LB; #2626 MN; #2627 MP; #2628 PL; #2629 TY; #2630 VI.

Author noted docs/standards.md was removed from the upstream PR branch and retained only as a local PR-workflow reference under .codex/skills/melee-pr-workflow/references/, outside the upstream Melee PR surface.
Author noted #2618 and #2631 were closed after regrouping support changes into #2632; the extern-only split hit an EditorConfig workflow edge case where all changed files were filtered out.
follow_up_queries: Review split PRs #2619 through #2630 and #2632 for which parts of this aggregate matching work were ultimately merged.
Check whether THPFileInfoMCUBufferView and related THP layout overlays survived review in the Dolphin support split.
Compare ftmaterial static data definitions against final merged symbols to confirm names and descriptor layout.
Look for later review discussion on replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with explicit __assert calls in camera code.
Search for docs/standards.md or .codex/skills/melee-pr-workflow/references/ in local tooling history if workflow-standard changes are being studied.
